api_kwargs.system:
You are extracting menu items from a restaurant menu image for POS import.
Read the image carefully — it is the primary source of truth.
OCR text is provided as a hint but often contains errors — always trust the image.

Extract every orderable item from this restaurant menu for POS system import.

Each item = one product a customer can order in a POS system.
Split compound items: "Beef or Chicken Empanadas" → two separate items.
Individual toppings, sauces, and dressings each get their own item.
Section-wide choices (e.g., "Naked or Breaded", "White or Wheat") → size variants on each item, not descriptions.
Section-wide notes (e.g., "All sandwiches come with lettuce, tomato...") → include in each item's description.
Use Title Case for names even if the menu is printed in ALL CAPS.

For each item return:
- "name": exact full name as printed on the menu
- "description": menu description if shown, null otherwise
- "price": price as float, 0 if not visible
- "category": one of: Pizza, Toppings, Appetizers, Salads, Soups, Sandwiches, Burgers, Wraps, Entrees, Seafood, Pasta, Steaks, Wings, Sauces, Sides, Desserts, Beverages, Kids Menu, Breakfast, Calzones, Subs, Platters, Other
- "sizes": [{"label": "...", "price": N.NN}] for items with multiple price points

Output ONLY valid JSON: {"items": [...]}

response.thinking_text:
Let me carefully analyze the menu image and extract all items. I'll go section by section.

**PIZZA**

First, the pizza sizes appear to be: 10" Med, 12" Set, 14" Lg, Family Size

**CHEESE** pizza - 8.00, 11.50, 13.95, 22.50
**EACH TOPPING ADD** - 1.50, 1.50, 2.25, 2.75

**PIZZA & CALZONE TOPPINGS:**
MEAT TOPPINGS: Pepperoni, Sausage, Hamburger, Bacon, Chicken, Pepperoni


VEGGIE TOPPINGS: Mushrooms, Fresh Tomatoes, Pineapple, Eggplant, Broccoli, Banana Peppers, Hot Peppers, Spinach, Black Olives, Onions, Roasted Red Peppers

They offer several sauce options including Garlic, Ranch, Red or Blue Cheese, White, Pesto, Alfredo, BBQ, and Honey BBQ. Now I'm moving into the gourmet pizza section, which has different sizing and includes specialty pizzas like the 4 Cheese with Ricotta and Mozzarella.

Looking at the rest of their gourmet lineup—they've got the Meat Lovers with pepperoni, sausage, bacon, ham and hamburger; a Combination with pepperoni, sausage, onions, peppers and mushrooms; a Mexican version with hamburger, hot peppers and cheddar; and a Hawaiian with pineapple, bacon and ham. There's also a Gyro Special that appears to continue beyond what I'm seeing. Continuing through the rest of the menu items with similar pricing tiers across small, medium, and large sizes. Looking at the rest of the pizza menu and now moving into the calzones section, which come in different sizes with consistent pricing tiers. The calzones are all stuffed with ricotta and mozzarella, served with sauce on the side, and include options like steak combo, buffalo chicken variations, and customizable builds. I'm looking at the calzone menu more carefully now - the cheese calzone has distinct small and large pricing at $9.50 and $12.95, while all the other varieties are consistently priced at $14.75 for small and $19.95 for large.

Now I'm reviewing the appetizers section, which includes garlic knots, various nacho options, fried sides like fries and onion rings, chicken tenders with different preparation styles, and items like mozzarella sticks, garlic bread, and stuffed mushrooms. I'm continuing through the appetizer menu, noting the pricing structure for items like the fried chicken platters, mozzarella sticks, and various potato skin options with their different toppings and portion sizes.

Now I'm looking more carefully at the menu image to verify the exact prices, and I can see the soups section listed at 6.50, followed by the fresh buffalo wings section with multiple flavor options available and different wing quantities priced at 9.95 for 6 pieces or 15.00 for 10 pieces, with the option to add fries. Looking at the burger variations with their regular and deluxe pricing, then moving into the club sandwich section with its fixed $14.00 price point and various protein options.

Now I'm seeing the melt sandwich category, which includes options like the Reuben with corned beef and sauerkraut, along with cheese and protein-based melts, each available in regular and deluxe sizes with customization options like adding bacon.

Moving into the wraps section, which offers gyros with beef or grilled chicken, tuna wraps, and various chicken options including ranch and buffalo variations with different heat levels and toppings.

Now I need to check the pricing structure for wraps—it looks like they list prices with fries included. Let me carefully go through each menu section again from the original image to make sure I'm capturing the details accurately before organizing everything into the JSON format.

Looking at the pizza sizes, I'm seeing four price columns: 10" Med, 12" (which might be "Set" or "Sml" depending on the pizza type), 14" Lg, and Family Size. For the gourmet pizzas specifically, it appears the sizes are 12" Sml, 14" Lg, and Family Size at 17x25. The calones have their own sizing with just Small and Large options.

Now I'm systematically extracting the pricing: Cheese pizza runs 8.00, 11.50, 13.95, and 22.50 across those sizes, with topping add-ons at 1.50, 1.50, 2.25, and 2.75 respectively. For gourmet pizzas, I'm seeing three sizes with prices like 14.75, 16.95, 25.95 for the 4 Cheese, and 17.95, 25 for the others—though I need to clarify which price corresponds to which size.

For wings, I'm dealing with flavor options (HOT, MILD, BBQ) and a Naked or Breaded choice that applies across the section, plus quantity-based pricing tiers. I'll treat the quantities as size variants and note the Naked or Breaded option in the item description rather than creating separate variants for each combination.

The wraps section has a similar situation with White or Wheat as a section-wide choice, so I need to handle that the same way.

Now I'm working through the rest of the menu systematically—burgers have Regular and Deluxe variants, club sandwiches are all $14.00, and melts also have Regular and Deluxe options. I'm noticing the wraps have dual pricing though: a base price and a separate "W/ FRIES" price, so I need to account for that distinction as I compile everything. Looking at the right side of the menu more carefully, I can see the burger and sandwich sections with their pricing structures—burgers have regular and deluxe options, sandwiches are listed at flat prices like $14.00, and wraps appear to follow a pattern where there's a base price and an additional charge for adding fries. I'm noticing that some items like soups might have a standard price across all varieties rather than individual pricing.

Now I need to track down the specific price for the spinach pie since it mentions the tzatziki sauce accompaniment, then I'll organize everything into a structured format.
